items.title: Initial thoughts:
items.author: Ryan Eichten
items.body_excerpt:
Initial thoughts:

-Setting everything aside, bummer to see Naz go. Was always great to talk too and of course the fans will miss him too

-I think in a vacuum, the Wolves did well value wise. Got a former All-Star with All-NBA upside and skills and LaMelo is a perfect fit need-wise (1/4)

items.title: You can also use courtsketch.com/shotmap to look up playoff shot maps like OG Anunoby who had one of the best playoff heaters of all time.
items.author: Hoop Informatics
items.body_excerpt:
You can also use courtsketch.com/shotmap to look up playoff shot maps like OG Anunoby who had one of the best playoff heaters of all time.

Red = above average
Blue = below average

images.alt:
Shot chart for OG Anunoby, 2025-26 NBA Postseason. 198 total shots. Overall eFG%: 67.2.

Court zones, frequency, and FG%:

Rim (under 8 feet): 41.9% of shots, 66.3% FG. Paint/floater (8-16 feet): 8.6% of shots, 41.2% FG. Midrange (16-24 feet): 4.0% of shots, 62.5% FG. Three-pointers (beyond 24 feet): 45.5% of shots, 48.9% FG.

Zone-by-zone 3PT breakdown: Left corner 42.9%, left wing 42.1%, top of key 55.6%, right wing 42.9%, right corner 59.3%.

Most hexagons are red (above league average), with scattered blue and teal hexagons (below average) primarily in the short midrange and floater zones. Shot volume is highest at the rim and along the 3PT arc.

Chart design by @kirkgoldsberry. Styling: @ofilispeaks. Automation: @GabeLeftBrain

items.title: Naz Reid 2025-26 Shot Map
items.author: Hoop Informatics
items.body_excerpt:
Naz Reid 2025-26 Shot Map

Red = above average
Blue = below average 

 —> courtsketch.com/shotmap

images.alt:
Shot chart for Naz Reid, 2025-26 NBA regular season. Overall eFG%: 54.9 on 871 shots. Color indicates efficiency relative to league average — blue is below average, red is above average. Hexagon size indicates shot frequency.

**Zone breakdown by frequency and FG%:**
Three-pointers (>24 ft) account for 51.4% of his shots at 36.2% FG%. Mid-range (16-24 ft) is nearly nonexistent at 0.3% frequency, 33.3% FG%. Short mid-range (8-16 ft) is 12.9% of shots at 40.2%. Rim attempts (<8 ft) make up 35.4% of shots at 61.4%.

**Directional 3PT breakdown:**
Far left: 40.6%. Left corner: 34.3%. Left wing: 34.1%. Top of arc: 31.3%. Right wing: 34.9%. Right corner: 34.5%. Far right: 42.3%.

**Paint zones:**
Short mid paint: 47.9%. Restricted area: 61.4%.

**Notable small samples:**
1 shot logged on the left mid-range extended area. 2 shots on the right wing extended area.

Chart styled by @ofilispeaks, automation by @GabeLeftBrain, original design by @kirkgoldsberry. Via courtsketch.com/shotmap.

items.title: LaMelo Ball 2025-26 Shot Map
items.author: Hoop Informatics
items.body_excerpt:
LaMelo Ball 2025-26 Shot Map

Red = above average
Blue = below average 

 —> courtsketch.com/shotmap

images.alt:
Shot chart for LaMelo Ball, 2025-26 NBA regular season. Overall eFG%: 51.6 on 1,244 shots. Color indicates efficiency relative to league average — blue is below average, red is above average. Hexagon size indicates shot frequency.

**Zone breakdown by frequency and FG%:**
Three-pointers (>24 ft) account for 59.5% of his shots at 36.8% FG%. Mid-range (16-24 ft) is nearly non-existent at 2.3% frequency, 46.4% FG%. Short mid-range (8-16 ft) is 13.3% of shots at 38.0%. Rim attempts (<8 ft) make up 24.9% of shots at 51.0%.

**Directional 3PT breakdown:**
Left wing: 37.0%. Left corner: 34.9%. Top of arc (center): 35.8%. Right wing: 35.1%. Right corner: 50.0% (3 shots). Far left: 42.1%. Far right: 43.1%.

**Paint zones:**
Short mid paint: 36.6%. Restricted area: 51.0%. Left elbow: 62.5% (small sample).

Chart styled by @ofilispeaks, automation by @GabeLeftBrain, original design by @kirkgoldsberry. Via courtsketch.com/shotmap.

items.title: I'm just being a stream of consciousness here, but for those of you asking:
items.author: Leo S
items.body_excerpt:
I'm just being a stream of consciousness here, but for those of you asking:

"So how are you gonna root for the Timberwolves now??"

I pretended like I celebrated the Derrick Rose 50-ball. I feigned intrigue when they signed Lance Stephenson to 10-days. LaMelo looks like a normal human next to them.

items.title: On one hand, LaMelo is:
items.author: Leo S
items.body_excerpt:
On one hand, LaMelo is:
-Rubio-esque passer
-Deadly deep 3P threat, albeit streaky
-Under 25 y/o
-Locked in for 3 yrs

But he's also:
-Likely a sociopath
-Huge traffic cone (pun intended)
-Degenerate stan following
-Super injury-prone
-Plays bball like a 10 y/o
-Very expensive
-Locked in for 3 yrs

items.title: The once supersized Timberwolves are now lacking size in the frontcourt.
items.author: Yossi Gozlan
items.body_excerpt:
The once supersized Timberwolves are now lacking size in the frontcourt.

They’ll be limited in ways to add to their frontcourt with few tradeable salaries and just 1 second-round pick to offer.

We could be looking at LaMelo-Ayo-Ant-Jaden-Gobert as the starting lineup.

items.title: I wouldn't be so focused on cramming big men into the Hornets newly created massive TPE (or cap space, if they go that route).
items.author: KeithSmithNBA
items.body_excerpt:
I wouldn't be so focused on cramming big men into the Hornets newly created massive TPE (or cap space, if they go that route).

Charlotte already had Moussa Diabate and Ryan Kalkbrenner (they love both). And they added Hannes Steinbach at this year draft. And they're adding Naz Reid in this trade.

items.title: June 25, 1999: San Antonio's Avery Johnson hits the game-winner with 0:47 left in Game 5 of the NBA Finals, and the Spurs win their first NBA Championship.
items.author: NBA Cobwebs
items.body_excerpt:
June 25, 1999: San Antonio's Avery Johnson hits the game-winner with 0:47 left in Game 5 of the NBA Finals, and the Spurs win their first NBA Championship.

Johnson had 9 AST and Tim Duncan scored 31 PTS in the 78-77 victory over the Knicks. Latrell Sprewell finished with 35 PTS/10 REB for New York.

items.title: While they didn't get him, Ball represents the type of swing the Raptors should make: plus-shooter, fits along Scottie's timeline, helps create and not just finish possessions.
items.author: Eric Koreen
items.body_excerpt:
While they didn't get him, Ball represents the type of swing the Raptors should make: plus-shooter, fits along Scottie's timeline, helps create and not just finish possessions. 

There aren't that many of those guys available, of course.

items.title: Philon, on fitting into the Sixers' defensive scheme: "Get steals and being able to pressure the ball at the point of attack. To be a guard in the NBA, you have to be able to play both sides...You have to be locked in, especially at the next level."
items.author: Derek Bodner
items.body_excerpt: Philon, on fitting into the Sixers' defensive scheme: "Get steals and being able to pressure the ball at the point of attack. To be a guard in the NBA, you have to be able to play both sides...You have to be locked in, especially at the next level."

items.title: Charlotte Hornets salary cap situation after trading LaMelo Ball and Josh Green for Naz Reid and picks.
items.author: Yossi Gozlan
items.body_excerpt:
Charlotte Hornets salary cap situation after trading LaMelo Ball and Josh Green for Naz Reid and picks.

$75 million in room below the luxury tax line.

Enough room to re-sign Coby White, use the entirety of their $40.8 million trade exception, and $15 million mid-level.

items.title: Labaron Philon, on returning for his sophomore season: "It was very important for me to go back, work on the shooting, and being able to play both on and off ball...That's one of my biggest traits, being able to adapt and do both."
items.author: Derek Bodner
items.body_excerpt: Labaron Philon, on returning for his sophomore season: "It was very important for me to go back, work on the shooting, and being able to play both on and off ball...That's one of my biggest traits, being able to adapt and do both."

items.title: The Minnesota Timberwolves project to be $4.7M over the luxury tax after adding LaMelo Ball and Josh Green.
items.author: KeithSmithNBA
items.body_excerpt:
The Minnesota Timberwolves project to be $4.7M over the luxury tax after adding LaMelo Ball and Josh Green.

Minnesota will be $3.3M under the first apron and $16.3M under the second apron, where they will be hard-capped due to aggregating together Julius Randle & Naz Reid as outgoing salary.

1/2

items.title: Some cap mechanic stuff from what will be a four-team trade between BKN-CHA-CHI-MIN:
items.author: KeithSmithNBA
items.body_excerpt:
Some cap mechanic stuff from what will be a four-team trade between BKN-CHA-CHI-MIN:

🏀No more $33M TPE for Julius Randle from the previously agreed to three-team trade. Wolves will instead use Randle's salary in aggregation to bring in LaMelo Ball/Josh Green

1/2

items.title: The ability to tweak and expand trades like this is why we do not update rosters and salaries for the trade machine until trades are official. We regularly get asked to push players to new teams based on agreed-to deals, but it would make it impossible to work something like this
items.author: KeithSmithNBA
items.body_excerpt: The ability to tweak and expand trades like this is why we do not update rosters and salaries for the trade machine until trades are official. We regularly get asked to push players to new teams based on agreed-to deals, but it would make it impossible to work something like this out.
